name: Google Workspace
summary: Gmail + Calendar + Drive + Sheets + Docs + Forms, driven by a power-agent: daily brief, inbox triage, scheduling, in-place spreadsheet/doc editing, per-client comms.
description: Connects Gmail, Google Calendar, Drive, Sheets, Docs, and Forms AND ships a Google power-agent (agents/google) that uses the WHOLE surface, not just read: inbox triage with labels + reply DRAFTS, free/busy scheduling via suggest_time, Drive search + permission checks, EDITING existing Google Sheets and Docs IN PLACE (update/append cells, insert/replace text -- so 'update this sheet' modifies the SAME file, keeping its ID/sharing/links, instead of spawning a new copy -- e.g. walk a contact sheet overnight and write researched emails back into the bounced rows), creating Google FORMS, and a daily brief. Email is DRAFT-FIRST by default (the agent composes, you send); auto-SEND is a documented one-flag opt-in (gmail:drafts -> gmail:send). It integrates with the rest of the platform: a client_map ties email/meetings/Drive to client folders (and files dated notes into each client's CLAUDE.md), and it drains the Calls (Granola) module's google destination into real calendar events. For a headless ClaudeFather the DEFAULT is Path B (a self-hosted Google MCP server with a stored refresh token -- acts for a dedicated/service account, no re-auth, can send); Path A (Claude's first-party connectors) is operator-account-bound and read/draft-only. The setup agent walks you through auth (incl. the remote-host reverse-tunnel case) + wiring the client_map so you never touch a raw config. NOTE: an install from before v2.2.0 must re-mint its token (re-consent) to grant the sheets/docs/forms scopes.

requires.label: A Google account + an OAuth Desktop client JSON from Google Cloud Console (the setup agent walks you through creating it -- project, enable APIs, Testing-mode consent + test-user, Desktop client; then mints the headless token for you)
